Trump has lost control of this war. It has no purpose. No endgame. It needs to stop.


Bloomberg (@business)

posts.text:
We are debating a bill this week to stop people from voting in part because the President wants the Senate to debate anything other than his deeply unpopular war, that has lit the Middle East on fire and is throwing global energy markets into chaos.

Video
posts.quote:

posts.text:
There are real crises in America today: spiraling costs, declining jobs, health premiums going up, billions wasted on an unnecessary war in Iran. 

But we aren't talking about any of that this month. All our time is being dedicated to a bill to stop citizens from voting. Absurd.

Video

posts.text:
Here's what I know.

The Iran War will cost $200B. Prices in the U.S. are spiking. The Middle East is on fire. A dozen soldiers are dead, more to come.

And for what? Iran's new leaders are worse than the old ones, and they will keep their missile, drone and nuclear programs.

Video

A group of us held a forum tonight with @votevets to talk about who will bear the cost of Trump's disastrous war with Iran. 

It won't be the Mar-a-Lago Epstein class. It will be poor and middle class families who can't afford gas and groceries and whose kids die in vain.

posts.text:
I will be on the floor with Cory to offer this resolution. There is a growing group of Democratic Senators who refuse to let Republicans keep the Senate silent on this incoherent, illegal, disastrous war.


Sen. Cory Booker (@SenBooker)

Tonight, on the Senate floor, I will call up my War Powers Resolution to withdraw U.S. forces from this reckless and unauthorized war of choice with Iran. 

The Senate must do its job and hold this administration accountable.



— https://nitter.net/SenBooker/st

posts.text:
We suffer a spiritual wound when prediction markets transform deep moral question of war and famine into a coarse money making opportunity. Not to mention the massive corruption it spurs inside government.

My bill with @RepCasar will ban prediction markets for government action.

Video
posts.quote:
We suffer a spiritual wound when prediction markets transform deep moral question of war and famine into a coarse money making opportunity. Not to mention the massive corruption it spurs inside government.

My bill with @RepCasar will ban prediction markets for government action.

Video

posts.text:
The day before the Iran War, a flurry of big prediction market bets were placed - likely from Trump staff - that the war would start the next day.

That's outrageous, and today @RepCasar and I introduced legislation to ban these corrupt prediction markets.

Video

Trump’s team of Epstein class advisors says it out loud more often than you’d think:

“consumers are the last of our concern right now”


Aaron Rupar (@atrupar)

Hassett: "If the war were to be extended, it wouldn't really disrupt the US economy very much at all. It would hurt consumers, and we'd have to think about what we'd have to do about that, but that's really the last of our concerns right now."

posts.text:
7/ The Strait of Hormuz is closed. Gas prices are spiking. Missiles rain down on Iran's neighbors. Iraq and Syria are teetering. And now war between Israel and Lebanon that will kill thousands and displace hundreds of thousands.

All the result of Trump's incompetence.
posts.quote:
7/ The Strait of Hormuz is closed. Gas prices are spiking. Missiles rain down on Iran's neighbors. Iraq and Syria are teetering. And now war between Israel and Lebanon that will kill thousands and displace hundreds of thousands.

All the result of Trump's incompetence.

posts.text:
6/ Israel needs to defend itself from Hezbollah rocket attacks. But a full ground invasion, modelled after Gaza, would likely be a strategic and humanitarian disaster. There are better options.

This could be Trump's legacy: a second Gaza.
reuters.com/world/middle-eas…

posts.text:
5/ But just like in Gaza, Israel risks throwing caution to the wind in a massive new invasion of southern Lebanon.

We are going to do [to Lebanon] what we did in Gaza," a senior Israeli official said.

Tens of thousands could die.

posts.text: 4/ But Trump threw it away with his attack on Iran. It was totally foreseeable that Iran would activate Hezbollah. Hezbollah started sending rockets into Israel. And the hardline Israeli government's reaction - total war with Lebanon - was foreseeable too.
posts.quote: 4/ But Trump threw it away with his attack on Iran. It was totally foreseeable that Iran would activate Hezbollah. Hezbollah started sending rockets into Israel. And the hardline Israeli government's reaction - total war with Lebanon - was foreseeable too.

posts.text: 3/ But Trump inherited something else good: a new Lebanese government - the most competent in decades - that was open to confronting Hezbollah. And this was the only real, long term solution - the Lebanese government taking full control of the country. nytimes.com/2026/03/06/world…
